rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PETER ZIDAN KWAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/4/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The amended petition was filed on 12/9/25 (e-filed on 12/10/25), which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. (2) The first accounting showed assets totaling $1,469,603.09 as of 1/29/23. Why does this amended accounting, beginning either 1/30/23 or 1/31/23, show assets totaling $1,380,103.19 (paragraph 15) or $1,429,103.19 (Schedule A - Summary of Account)? (3) Where in the first accounting was the $332.84 "additional property received" shown? There was no detailed schedule in that accounting showing this additional property. (4) Paragraph 26 shows the fee calculation beginning with a date of death value of the estate that was not the value shown in the I&As. (5) If copies or printouts of bank statements are filed (attached to an e-filed petition), the fiduciary must verify that they are original. [See language in CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts.] Please provide a revised proposed order (if applicable) and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 2/20/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The rest of the accounting has not been fully reviewed, given the incorrect starting value.)

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MALEE TANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/10/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No proof of service has been filed. Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) Proof of payment of the assessment fee must be filed before an accounting can be approved. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any other filed papers to the assigned examiner by 2/24/26. The petition has not yet been reviewed, as no courtesy copy was provided. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF RUANGNAM PONGGLUD
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the notice of hearing did not have a printout of the instructions for how to appear remotely attached to it [LRSF 14.1 C.6.], and the proposed order and courtesy copies were provided after the deadline. The executor apparently did not invest the cash in an interest-bearing accounts, as alleged in paragraph 11, as the cash on hand is exactly the same as on the date of death. Translations must set for the translator's qualifications and/or credentials. [LRSF 14.15 D.] NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] An order should not include findings not disclosed in a verified pleading.

rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/21/26. Examiner's Notes emailed to counsel with the following issues: 1. Item 6 is incomplete. 2. Item 7 is incomplete. 3. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestors through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named and their relationships to the decedent and dates of deaths need to be stated [Local Rule 14.18C]. Please revise Item 8 of Petition.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN THOMAS HIGGINS REED
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/21/2026. The following issues must be resolved at least two weeks before this date. Petitioner's verifications are required in clearing the issues. (1) An amended petition requires a new notice and a new publication. CRC 7.53(a). (2) Please make clear what the decedent's name is. The will says JOHN T. REED, but the petition and publication use JOHN THOMAS HIGGINS REED only. If the petitioner intends to use both names, an amended petition and re-notice with all names may be needed. (3) Petition #8 must identify the trustee of the sole devisee trust. If the petitioner is the trustee, all trust beneficiaries must be listed and properly notified. Prob. Code sections 1208, 8110. Local Rule 14.18.C. (4) Petition #8 to indicate who David R. Kennedy is. Notice was given to him; however, the Petition is silent. (5) Notice of Directions for Remote appearance and Access must be given to the decedent's father/heir. LR 14.1.C. (6) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number [DE-147S], filed on December 11, 2025, is incomplete. LR 14.18.E.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GREGORY A. MARTIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues to be cleared or addressed: (1) Petition #8 only lists children or descendants of the pre-deceased siblings without stating if there has been a probate proceeding of each pre-deceased sibling. If there has, the 13100 affidavit seems inappropriate. Without this information, the examiner cannot fully review whether everyone who should get notice actually did. The petitioner is to file a verified supplement on this issue. (2) Even if the people listed on Petition #8 are the correct heirs, the waivers of bond on file look incomplete because #4 does not include the name of the person who will be the administrator. Supplement filed 12/17/25 states the petitioner's name, but the supplement is from the petitioner, not the persons who are waiving bond. The Court will determine its sufficiency. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

Section 12200 (requesting additional time for estate administration) at least two weeks before 6/22/27. Appearance will be required on that status date if no petition has been filed.
